title: Senior Product Manager, Claude
score_rationale: Strong AI/ML domain alignment — your search platform experience maps directly to building LLM-powered products. Samsung and Amazon search roles show exactly the data-driven PM profile Anthropic looks for. Minor gap: no direct safety/alignment background, but quantified ML impact covers 80% of requirements.
recommendation_reason: Top-tier AI company with direct domain match; your search + ML PM background is a strong fit for Claude product work.
suggested_summary: Senior PM with 7+ years leading AI/ML-powered search and discovery products at scale (10M+ MAU, $2B+ GMV impact). Track record shipping data-driven features that measurably improve user outcomes through ML and experimentation.
top_bullets: Led AI-powered search platform serving 10M+ monthly users, improving CTR by 23%
$2B+ GMV search relevance at Amazon, shipped with cross-functional ML teams
Built real-time personalization engine increasing conversion by 18%
gap_handling: Acknowledge no direct safety research background; emphasize genuine interest in responsible AI and frame LLM product work as adjacent context.

title: Product Manager, Guest Discovery and Homepage
location: San Francisco CA, Los Angeles CA, New York NY
score_rationale: Riley's experience leading AI-powered search and discovery at Samsung.com — including hybrid search, personalization, recommendation systems, and conversational AI — maps almost directly to Airbnb's homepage discovery mandate. The role explicitly calls for incorporating AI/relevance models into browsing experiences, which is the core of Riley's current PM III scope serving 10M+ monthly users. The one mild gap is consumer travel marketplace context and lifecycle/growth marketing integration, which Riley hasn't held directly but has adjacency to via CRM and CDP work at Kolon.
recommendation_reason: Near-perfect domain match — AI-powered discovery, search relevance, personalization, and large-scale consumer product ownership all align directly with the role's requirements at a top-tier brand.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years specializing in search, discovery, and personalized consumer experiences at scale — including leading AI-powered homepage and search for Samsung.com's 10M+ monthly users. Proven track record shipping hybrid search, recommendation systems, and conversational AI that drive measurable engagement, conversion, and revenue growth in competitive e-commerce and marketplace environments.
top_bullets: Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation CSAT (NPS) by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion.

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data; launched an add-ons discovery page boosting AOV by 15%, with end-to-end delivery from ideation through production.
gap_handling: Riley lacks direct travel/hospitality marketplace context and hands-on lifecycle/growth marketing campaign ownership. Address this in cover letter by drawing parallels between e-commerce discovery funnels and travel intent browsing, and cite the CDP + CRM personalization work at Kolon as evidence of cross-channel marketing alignment.

title: Senior Product Manager, AI (Customer Agent)
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background — especially conversational AI, LLM pipelines, and retrieval-adjacent work at Samsung — maps closely to Klaviyo's Customer Agent stack (RAG, agent architectures, LLMs). The conversational AI chat experience shipped at Samsung and the AEO pipeline are strong direct parallels to agent configuration and deployment. The main gap is B2B SaaS platform experience: Riley's background is predominantly B2C e-commerce with one B2B stint at Amazon (seller tools), whereas this role spans brand-facing configuration systems, onboarding funnels, and growth loops in a SaaS context — a meaningful but bridgeable stretch.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/conversational product fit but role skews B2B SaaS platform — requires tailoring to bridge e-commerce background to brand-operator product surface; also, Boston location may be a friction point.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered and conversational AI experiences at Samsung and Amazon, including end-to-end ownership of an on-site conversational AI agent and an automated AI pipeline that drove 8x agent traffic growth. Proven track record of bridging complex AI/ML systems with intuitive user experiences, defining success metrics, and leading cross-functional teams to deliver measurable business outcomes at scale.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%
gap_handling: The biggest gap is B2B SaaS brand-operator experience — Riley's products were primarily built for consumers, not for business users configuring and deploying tools. In the resume and cover letter, lean into the Amazon B2B seller tools role (2M+ sellers, workflow automation, onboarding optimization) as proof of building for operator/brand personas. Also explicitly call out familiarity with RAG and agent architectures in the resume skills section — these are screening keywords for this role.

title: Product Manager, API Growth
location: Remote-Friendly (Travel-Required) | San Francisco, CA | Seattle, WA; San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley is a strong AI/Search PM with 9 years of experience and impressive measurable impact across Samsung, Amazon, and Kolon — the AI domain and technical chops align well with Anthropic's platform. However, this role is specifically a **Growth PM for a developer/API platform**, which is a distinct archetype from Riley's core AI Search and E-commerce Discovery background. Riley has growth signals (funnel optimization, A/B testing, activation/monetization metrics) but limited direct experience with developer-facing products, API platforms, or B2B SaaS growth loops. The Amazon B2B seller tooling role is the closest proxy, but it lasted under a year and was more automation/listing workflow than developer platform growth.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI and experimentation background is a great foundation, but the developer/API growth PM archetype is a notable gap that requires deliberate resume tailoring and a compelling cover narrative.
suggested_summary: Product Manager with 9+ years driving growth for AI-powered products at scale, including A/B-tested search and discovery systems serving 10M+ monthly users and automated ML pipelines for 2M+ B2B users. Passionate about building at the frontier of AI, with a track record of owning full-funnel growth metrics — acquisition, activation, and monetization — across cross-functional teams in fast-paced environments.
top_bullets: Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines across 20+ categories

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, leading a phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels, increasing web navigation CSAT (NPS) by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks
gap_handling: The biggest gap is developer-platform / API growth experience. In your cover letter and resume tailoring, reframe the Amazon role explicitly around "self-serve B2B platform growth" — highlight the 80% reduction in listing time as activation improvement, the $1.4M cost savings as monetization efficiency, and the 380bps EoU score increase as an onboarding funnel win. Also surface your AEO pipeline work as proof of API-adjacent product thinking (structured data extraction, pipeline delivery, developer-side outputs). Avoid overstating; instead, position your background as "consumer + B2B growth + AI" and make the case that Anthropic's Claude Platform sits at exactly that intersection.

title: Staff Product Manager, Search Algorithms
location: New York City, NY; San Francisco, CA
score_rationale: Riley's core competency in AI-powered search, relevance ranking, and experimentation maps almost perfectly to Faire's Search Algorithms charter — the Samsung work alone (hybrid search, precision@3, behavioral signals, 10M+ users) reads like a direct audition for this role. The main friction is seniority: Faire is asking for Staff PM with 7+ years and signals founder/CEO-level exposure, which is a half-step stretch above Riley's current PM III title, and the B2B marketplace domain (wholesale, retailer-facing) is a slight pivot from B2C e-commerce. The NYC/SF in-office hybrid requirement also creates a relocation consideration from Bellevue, though SF is a listed preferred city.
recommendation_reason: Exceptional search/AI domain match and strong evidence bullets, but Staff-level seniority is a stretch and the B2B wholesale context requires deliberate positioning — apply with a tightly tailored narrative.
suggested_summary: Senior Product Manager with 9 years of experience building AI-powered search and discovery systems at scale — including hybrid search across international marketplaces, ranking optimization with behavioral signals, and ML-driven personalization on platforms serving 10M+ monthly users. Proven operator who partners deeply with data science and engineering to define metrics, run rigorous experiments, and ship algorithm improvements that directly lift engagement and revenue.
top_bullets: Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users
gap_handling: The Staff PM bar and B2B wholesale context are the two real gaps. Address seniority by leading with scope (10M+ users, global rollouts, CEO/executive alignment at Samsung) rather than title. For the B2B angle, draw a tight parallel: Amazon's 2M+ B2B seller work demonstrates you understand supply-side catalog complexity, and Faire's "retailer as customer" model mirrors that dynamic. Frame your Samsung search work as serving a two-sided discovery problem (shopper + product catalog) — structurally identical to Faire's retailer-brand marketplace.

title: Principal, Product Manager - AI / LLM
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/LLM product credentials — conversational AI, RAG-adjacent search pipelines, LLM-driven AEO, and recommendation systems — that map meaningfully to Twilio's Copilot backend vision. The domain alignment is solid on the AI/NLP side, and the conversational AI chat experience at Samsung is a direct proof point. However, the role skews heavily toward backend platform/infrastructure PM work (RAG pipelines, LLM orchestration, API reliability, latency management) and developer/B2B tooling, which is a partial mismatch with Riley's consumer-facing search and e-commerce discovery background. The seniority designation — "Principal PM" — is one level above Riley's target, making this a stretch hire.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM signal and conversational AI proof points qualify Riley, but the Principal-level seniority, backend platform focus, and developer-persona requirement are meaningful gaps to address in tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years of experience shipping LLM-driven pipelines, conversational AI experiences, and intelligent search systems at scale for 10M+ users. Proven track record designing AI backend logic — from RAG-style knowledge retrieval to agentic discovery flows — that reduce friction, drive engagement, and deliver measurable business impact.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: The role's core ask — RAG pipeline ownership, LLM latency/cost management, and backend agent orchestration — is more infrastructure-PM than Riley's current portfolio shows. In the resume and cover letter, lean into the AEO pipeline (structured data extraction → schema markup → AI agent traffic) as a RAG-adjacent proof point, and the conversational AI chat launch as evidence of agent product thinking. Be explicit about technical depth with LLMs and frame the Samsung search ranking work as backend signal-processing experience.

title: Staff Product Manager, AI Features
score_rationale: Good AI domain alignment and remote flexibility. Figma's AI features team is building generative design tools — your ML product experience is relevant, but design tool domain is new territory. Staff-level role is a stretch given target Senior PM level.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI PM match, but design domain is unfamiliar and Staff level is a stretch — worth applying if you can connect to design workflow context.
suggested_summary: Senior PM with deep AI/ML product delivery experience and a track record of leading cross-functional teams to ship data-driven features. Excited to bring search + personalization expertise to AI-powered creative tooling.
top_bullets: Led AI-powered search platform serving 10M+ monthly users, improving CTR by 23%
Partnered with data science teams to build real-time recommendation systems
Drove A/B experimentation framework reducing feature release cycle by 40%
gap_handling: Design tool domain gap is real — acknowledge it, but pivot to how ML/search PM skills (user behavior signals, relevance, recommendations) transfer directly to AI feature work in any domain.

title: Senior Product Manager, Notion AI
score_rationale: Notion AI is building LLM-powered writing and knowledge management features. Your AI/ML product experience is directionally right, but consumer productivity SaaS is a different motion than ecommerce search. NY location is a match. Score reflects domain gap rather than skill gap.
recommendation_reason: Good AI fit and NYC location match, but consumer SaaS / productivity domain is new — tailor your application to highlight user behavior and growth metrics.
suggested_summary: Senior PM with 7+ years shipping AI/ML-powered products at scale. Experience using quantitative signals and experimentation to drive discovery and engagement — skills that transfer directly to AI-powered knowledge tools.
top_bullets: Led AI-powered search and discovery platform serving 10M+ monthly users
Drove AEO strategy improving organic discovery by 35%
Built personalization engine increasing conversion rate by 18%
gap_handling: Consumer SaaS / productivity domain is less familiar than ecommerce — frame your experience around user intent, discovery, and engagement patterns, which are common threads.

title: Product Manager, Claude Code
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials and a proven track record of shipping intelligent systems at scale — directly relevant to Anthropic's mission. However, the Claude Code role is specifically a developer tools / dev tooling PM position requiring hands-on engineering background (1+ years as a professional engineer) and experience with CLI products, coding tools, and developer ecosystems — none of which appear in Riley's resume. Riley's domain is AI-powered e-commerce search and discovery, not developer-facing or platform tooling, which creates a meaningful archetype mismatch. The role's $285–305K comp band and Anthropic brand signal a Senior/Staff-level position, but the engineering background requirement is a hard gap Riley will struggle to paper over.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI product credentials and Anthropic sponsorship fit, but the required engineering background and dev tools domain are significant gaps Riley must address head-on.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping AI-powered systems — from LLM-driven pipelines and conversational AI to hybrid search and recommendation models — in close partnership with ML researchers and engineering teams. Passionate about translating frontier model capabilities into intuitive, high-impact product experiences and bringing the same rigor to developer-facing AI tooling.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks
gap_handling: The biggest gap is the explicit requirement for 1+ year as a professional engineer. Riley should address this directly in a cover letter — either by surfacing any coding/engineering work done during or adjacent to PM roles (SQL, data pipeline work, API design familiarity), highlighting how closely they've embedded with engineering teams to co-design technical systems, or noting hands-on use of AI coding tools like Claude Code, Cursor, or GitHub Copilot. If Riley has any side projects, scripts, or technical coursework from the Purdue MSBA program, this is the place to mention them.

title: Product Manager, Consumer
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product credentials — hybrid search, LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and personalization at scale — align strongly with Anthropic's Consumer team domain. However, the role has an unusually specific signal: it explicitly seeks a "former consumer founder" with 0-to-1 product development experience and prototype-to-launch instincts, which Riley's resume doesn't directly demonstrate. The seniority fit is also a concern: the $385K–$460K comp band and the "former founder" language suggest this is a Staff/Principal-level IC role, sitting above Riley's target Senior PM tier, and Riley's experience has been primarily within large enterprises (Samsung, Amazon) rather than in scrappy consumer product ideation environments.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI domain alignment and proven consumer product metrics, but the "former founder" and 0-to-1 emphasis is a meaningful gap Riley's resume doesn't clearly address — apply only with significant resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping consumer-facing AI experiences — from conversational search and LLM-driven pipelines to personalized discovery at 10M+ monthly users — with a track record of identifying emerging capabilities and taking products from zero to launch. Passionate about building AI products that genuinely improve daily life, with deep cross-functional experience bridging research, engineering, and design to deliver measurable outcomes.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience — from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch — in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion
gap_handling: The "former consumer founder" requirement is the hardest gap to close on paper. Riley should directly address this in a cover letter — emphasizing the entrepreneurial scope of Samsung.com projects (e.g., building net-new products like the AEO pipeline and conversational chat from scratch, owning 0-to-1 scope within a large org). If Riley has any side projects, open-source tools, or personal AI prototypes built with LLMs, those must be prominently featured — even in an "Other Projects" resume section — to signal the founder-like, hands-on prototyping instinct this role demands.

title: Product Manager, Monetization
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI product and e-commerce growth credentials — A/B testing, conversion optimization, funnel work, and personalization — that partially map to this role. However, the core ask is monetization-specific PM work: subscription pricing & packaging, free-to-paid conversion funnels, and consumption-based billing at scale, which are not explicitly represented in Riley's resume. The Amazon and Samsung experience shows growth-adjacent skills, but the resume reads more as AI Search / Discovery than Growth / Monetization, which is the primary archetype this role demands.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI domain and growth adjacency, but missing direct monetization (pricing, subscription conversion, billing) ownership — a reframing of existing work is needed before applying.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving measurable revenue growth through data-driven experimentation, conversion funnel optimization, and personalized product experiences at Samsung and Amazon. Proven track record launching AI-powered features — from recommendation systems and conversational AI to search ranking — that improve engagement, conversion, and monetization outcomes at scale for millions of users.
top_bullets: Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing
Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, increasing web navigation CSAT score (NPS) by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users
Restructured new customer benefit package and revamped onboarding workflow, increasing first purchase rate by 30% and boosting 1-year retention by 7% and re-purchase rate by 15%
gap_handling: Riley's resume lacks explicit monetization vocabulary (pricing tiers, paywall optimization, free-to-paid conversion, subscription lifecycle). Before applying, reframe the Kolon onboarding/retention work and Samsung AEO/conversion work in monetization language — e.g., "drove paid conversion," "optimized upgrade funnel," "improved revenue per user." title: Staff Product Manager, Lyft AI Platform & Marketplace Applications
score_rationale: Riley's 9 years of AI/ML product experience — particularly hybrid search, LLM pipelines, and recommendation systems — maps well to the AI platform domain, and her Samsung and Amazon experience shows strong cross-functional leadership at scale. However, this role is explicitly a Staff PM focused on an internal AI Platform (agentic systems, multi-agent orchestration, platform adoption/evangelism) rather than consumer-facing search or discovery products, which is a meaningful archetype mismatch. The agentic AI and internal platform tooling angle is a significant gap in Riley's resume as written, and the role sits one level above her target seniority (Staff vs. Senior/PM III).
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML product credentials and analytics depth are compelling, but the agentic platform archetype and Staff seniority are meaningful stretches requiring deliberate resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping production-grade AI/ML systems — including LLM-driven pipelines, hybrid search, and recommendation platforms — at Samsung, Amazon, and high-growth e-commerce. Proven track record driving cross-functional adoption of AI capabilities at scale, defining platform metrics, and translating complex technical systems into measurable business outcomes for executive stakeholders.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: The biggest gap is explicit experience with agentic AI systems and internal platform PM work (evangelist/adoption role vs. user-facing product). Address this by reframing the AEO pipeline and conversational AI chat bullets to emphasize pipeline architecture, internal stakeholder adoption, and multi-system orchestration language. If you have any exposure to agent frameworks (LangChain, AutoGen, etc.) or internal tooling evangelism, add it — even in a "Familiar" skills caveat.

title: Senior Product Manager, Project & Task Experience
score_rationale: Riley meets the seniority bar (5+ years, currently PM III) and demonstrates strong cross-functional execution and data-driven product delivery — directly matching Asana's stated process expectations. However, the core domain is enterprise B2B work management / productivity software (project & task UX), which is meaningfully different from Riley's AI search, e-commerce discovery, and marketplace toolkit background. The "complex data models and visualizations" and "human-centric UX for enterprise compliance" requirements are areas where Riley's resume provides limited direct evidence.
recommendation_reason: Strong PM fundamentals and cross-functional execution match, but domain gap in B2B enterprise productivity/work management software requires deliberate framing to be competitive.
suggested_summary: Senior Product Manager with 9+ years shipping high-impact product experiences at scale, including enterprise-facing B2B tools at Amazon (2M+ sellers) and AI-powered discovery systems serving 10M+ monthly users. Known for translating ambiguous customer problems into clear, data-driven roadmaps and partnering tightly with engineering, design, and data science to deliver intuitive, measurable product improvements.
top_bullets: Saved $1.4M in operational cost by enhancing self-serve workflow for 2M+ B2B sellers at Amazon, reducing manual contacts with support agents through 10+ interviews, 300+ surveys, and coordination across 5 dependent teams.

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, leading phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels — increasing NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% via multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines across 20+ categories.
gap_handling: The biggest gap is enterprise productivity/work management domain experience — Asana explicitly prefers B2B platform or enterprise background with complex data models and UX. Riley should lean hard into the Amazon role (B2B seller platform, complex catalog data models, enterprise-scale self-serve UX) and frame Samsung.com as serving enterprise-level stakeholders with compliance-adjacent requirements. Avoid leading with e-commerce consumer framing; reposition as "platform thinking at scale."

title: Senior Product Manager - Data Agent
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product depth, SQL fluency, LLM/conversational AI experience, and strong experimentation track record are genuine assets for this role. However, the job has a heavy B2B SaaS and modern data stack emphasis — BI tools, semantic layers, data warehouses, governance consoles — which is largely absent from Riley's background, which skews consumer e-commerce and B2C search. The 0-to-1 entrepreneurial framing and Datadog's observability-first context also push toward a B2B/enterprise PM archetype that is a stretch for Riley's profile.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM and data product credentials, but limited B2B SaaS and modern data stack (BI, semantic layer, warehouse) domain experience creates a meaningful gap for this enterprise-focused 0-to-1 role.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered search, conversational AI, and data-driven personalization systems at Samsung and Amazon. Proven track record defining eval frameworks, instrumentation strategies, and AI product roadmaps that deliver measurable impact — bringing the analytical rigor and cross-functional ownership needed to build Datadog's Data Agent from zero to one.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata (product attributes, inventory, pricing) from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Defined success metrics for search engagement and led instrumentation strategy in partnership with engineering and analytics teams. Leveraged behavioral insights to iterate search experiences, increasing search success rate by 15% and CTR by 25% QoQ
gap_handling: Riley lacks direct BI/semantic layer and data warehouse domain experience. To close the gap before applying: (1) Study the modern data stack landscape — dbt, Snowflake, Looker/LookML semantic models, and tools like Cube or AtScale — and be ready to discuss them intelligently. (2) Reframe the AEO pipeline and structured catalog metadata work as "semantic layer thinking" — extracting, structuring, and governing data for downstream consumption. (3) Lean hard on the Amazon B2B experience (2M+ sellers, ML-driven pipelines, structured catalog data) to demonstrate B2B SaaS instincts. (4) Highlight SQL proficiency and BigQuery use prominently — this role explicitly asks for it.

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
location: Bellevue, Washington; Chicago, Illinois; New York, New York; Washington, DC
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and agentic-adjacent work (AEO pipelines, multi-agent workflow familiarity) — which partially satisfy the AI/LLM requirement. However, the role's core requirement is deep Identity & Access Management (IAM) expertise — OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust, agent authentication/authorization — which is absent from Riley's resume entirely. This is a developer-platform / security-infrastructure PM role, not a search/discovery or e-commerce role, so domain alignment is also a significant gap. The seniority level and AI experience are genuine assets, but the IAM deficit is hard to bridge without direct experience.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product experience is a genuine asset, but the complete absence of IAM/security domain knowledge is a serious gap for a role where OAuth2/OIDC/SAML is listed as a hard requirement.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years building LLM-powered pipelines, conversational AI systems, and agentic data workflows at Samsung and Amazon, delivering measurable impact at scale for 10M+ users. Eager to apply deep AI product expertise to the emerging AI identity space, bridging secure agent authentication with real-world agentic orchestration frameworks.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: The IAM gap is the elephant in the room — address it head-on in a cover letter by framing your AEO pipeline work (structured data extraction, agent traffic governance) and conversational AI scope as hands-on experience with non-human entity workflows. Demonstrate you've self-studied OAuth2/OIDC basics and can speak to token exchange concepts. Lean heavily into the "Zero to One" preferred qualification — your Samsung search vision and Kolon live commerce launches are strong examples of 0→1 delivery.

title: Senior Product Manager
score_rationale: Riley's 9 years in product with deep AI, search/discovery, and personalization experience maps well to the role's AI-powered Help Center and discovery-led transformation mandate. However, this role is fundamentally about support enablement, help-center UX, and CustOps tooling — domains Riley has no direct experience in — making it a partial fit rather than a natural match. The Growth context and AI self-service angle are genuine overlaps, but the absence of customer support platform experience and the NYC hybrid requirement (Riley is in Bellevue, WA) are notable friction points.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/search/discovery overlap with the role's vision, but no direct support-enablement or help-center domain experience, and the NYC hybrid requirement is a relocation ask for a Bellevue-based candidate.
suggested_summary: Product leader with 9+ years shipping AI-powered discovery, search, and conversational experiences at scale — including AI chat, hybrid search, and personalized recommendation systems used by 10M+ monthly users. Proven track record of transforming fragmented product surfaces into cohesive, metrics-driven experiences that improve engagement, reduce friction, and accelerate conversion across the full user lifecycle.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users
Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams, increasing web navigation CSAT (NPS) by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users
Streamlined product discovery funnels by launching category-specific finder experiences and optimizing taxonomy, increasing PDP view rate by 45% and reducing bounce rate by 50%
gap_handling: Riley has no direct resume evidence of help-center, knowledge-base, or CustOps tooling work. To bridge this gap, the cover letter should explicitly draw a parallel: the Samsung.com conversational AI chat and AEO pipeline work are both about deflecting friction and helping users self-serve — the same core objective as a Help Center. Frame the search/discovery redesign at Samsung as a "discovery-led platform transformation," which directly mirrors the JD's language. Acknowledge the domain shift as a feature, not a bug — bringing AI search and personalization rigor to a space that has historically lacked it.

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — LLM pipelines, conversational AI, agentic features, and a track record of shipping at scale — which directly addresses the AI half of this role. However, the core requirement is deep Identity and Access Management (IAM) expertise (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust, token exchange), a domain where Riley has no visible experience. This is a platform/developer-tooling PM role for enterprise security infrastructure, which is a meaningful archetype departure from Riley's consumer/e-commerce discovery background. The AI signal is strong, but the IAM gap is a hard blocker for most hiring managers.
recommendation_reason: Compelling AI/LLM product experience bridges half the requirement, but zero IAM/security protocol background is a significant gap for a role where OAuth2/OIDC expertise is listed as required.
suggested_summary: Senior Product Manager with 9+ years driving AI-powered product experiences — including LLM pipelines, conversational AI agents, and agentic discovery systems — at Samsung and Amazon. Proven ability to ship zero-to-one AI features at enterprise scale, working cross-functionally with ML engineers, architects, and data scientists to deliver measurable business impact.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: Be upfront in the cover letter: acknowledge the IAM/OAuth2 learning curve and frame it as a strength — you've shipped production AI/agentic systems and understand the "agent" side deeply, and you're coming in to own the AI product surface while ramping on identity protocols. Consider doing a 1-week crash course on OAuth2/OIDC/SAML and referencing that in your application (e.g., "currently deepening knowledge of OAuth2/OIDC as it applies to non-human identities"). Do NOT claim IAM experience you don't have.

title: Senior Product Manager, APM - Observability
score_rationale: This role is firmly in the DevOps/Observability domain — APM, distributed tracing, Synthetics, and RUM — which has virtually no overlap with Riley's core expertise in AI search, e-commerce discovery, and personalization. While Riley demonstrates strong PM fundamentals (A/B testing, cross-functional leadership, data-driven iteration) and has worked with AI/ML at scale, none of her experience directly addresses application performance monitoring, distributed tracing, synthetic transaction monitoring, or RUM. The "Search AI" branding at Elastic is enticing surface-level alignment, but this specific role is an observability infrastructure play, not a search or consumer AI product.
recommendation_reason: Deep APM/Observability domain expertise is the core requirement — Riley has no demonstrable experience in distributed tracing, synthetic monitoring, or RUM, making this a significant domain mismatch.
suggested_summary: Senior Product Manager with 9+ years driving AI-powered product experiences at scale, including search, data pipelines, and ML-driven instrumentation. Proven track record of defining success metrics, leading instrumentation strategies, and partnering with engineering and data science to deliver measurable outcomes across complex technical domains.
top_bullets: Defined success metrics for search engagement and led instrumentation strategy in partnership with engineering and analytics teams — increasing search success rate by 15% and CTR by 25% QoQ

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering on multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: If Riley wants to pursue Elastic, she should target roles on the Search AI or Vector Search teams — where her hybrid search, semantic search, and LLM product experience maps directly. For this specific APM role, the gap is structural: APM and observability are a distinct technical discipline requiring hands-on experience with tracing pipelines, telemetry agents, and developer tooling that isn't bridgeable through resume tailoring alone.
